field_evidence.critical_condition_basis: The mobile phase composition to reach the critical conditions was determined to be 58.05% chloroform, 6.45% methanol, and 35.50% n-heptane (v/v/v).
field_evidence.critical_component: Critical conditions for the PEO were determined using Nucleosil (highly deactivated) C8 columns
field_evidence.column_name: Macherey-Nagel 250 mm x 4.6 mm Nucleosil C8 HD
field_evidence.mobile_phase_solvents: mixture of three organics solvents (58.05 vol % of chloroform, 6.45 vol % of methanol, and 35.50 vol % of n-heptane
field_evidence.temperature_celsius: temperature of 30 °C was maintained for all measurements

conditions.notes: Elution mechanism governed by interaction of polar endgroup with residual free silanols. PEO block is invisible in block copolymers.
conditions.physical_address: Aix-Marseille Univ, CNRS, Institut de Chimie Radicalaire, UMR 7273, Faculte de Saint-Jerome, avenue Escadrille Normandie-Niemen, service 542, 13397 Marseille cedex 20, France

conditions.critical_condition_basis: elution independent of molar mass of PEO block

field_evidence.critical_condition_basis: In these conditions, the PEO block is then invisible
field_evidence.column_name: Macherey-Nagel 250 mm x 4.6 mm Nucleosil C8 HD
field_evidence.mobile_phase_solvents: mixture of three organics solvents (58.05 vol % of chloroform, 6.45 vol % of methanol, and 35.50 vol % of n-heptane
field_evidence.temperature_celsius: temperature of 30 °C was maintained for all measurements

conditions.notes: PS block elutes in SEC mode. Calibration curve from PS homopolymers used to determine PS block molar mass.
